description: Letter from ʿEli b. Yehezqel ha-Kohen, Jerusalem, to Eli b. Hayyim ha-Kohen, Fustat, concerning Eli b. Yehezqel's travels to Acre and Tyre, return to Ramla, and his serious illness there. "I was attacked by vomiting; I exploded from above and below, over 300 times (lit. "sittings"); everybody in the house gave me up. The doctors came on Saturday night and saw that I was finished and that there was nothing to be done any more. I made my will... [then improved]... but I still have terrible weakness." Also mentions the rent from the heqdesh (pious foundation) to the poor people in Jerusalem and other private and public affairs. 1060 (Gil's estimation). (Information from Gil, Palestine; and Goitein's index cards, including index card #27100.) VMR; ASE.
tags: waqf, Jerusalem, Illness: treatment, Illness: physician, Tyre, Illness, Ramla, Acre, illness: despair, illness: rich description, illness letter 969-1517, illness: gastrointestinal, qodesh
description_he: מכתב מאלי בן יחזקאל הכהן, ירושלים, לאלי בן חיים הכהן, פוסטאט. מספר על נסיעות, מחלה קשה ו-300 הקאות. 1060.
